text: Hello, here we see a laparoscopic cholecystectomy to lift the rectum that is filled with rectal stones that cause the upper right abdomen to rupture repeatedly and for quite a long time. Here it looks like there are many joints that surround the rectum, so it must be released to achieve an important structure when performing a cholecystectomy.

such as SysTick Duck and SysTick Artery. The bonding can be freed by using an energy device such as a mono-counter that is connected to a fruit in Zubat, Maryland, or by using a harmonic snowball as shown in the video now.

The surgeon carefully dissects to isolate the cystic duct. Once isolated, the cystic duct is clipped three times: two clips are placed proximally on the shoulder of the duct, and one clip is placed distally.
The surgeon prepares to transect or cut the cystic duct, ensuring the operating field is clear by cleaning the blood vessels for better visibility.
The surgeon isolates the cystic artery, applying clips to secure it before transecting the artery to separate it from surrounding tissue.
Those are the important points that are done when laparoscopic cholecystectomy. I hope this video is useful to give you insight. Thank you for watching. Greetings.
